revision.text:
[[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Jinguji Temple.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|qid=Q10343918|1=Tōji Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Futenma|qid=Q11514557|1=Futenma}}|coordinates={{Coord|26.29275|N|127.776806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Refimprove|date=February 28, 2014}} {{Infobox religious building |name = Jingū-ji |image = File:Jinguji Temple.jpg |caption = Jingū-ji (January 2023) |location = 1-27-11 {{ill|Futenma|ja|普天間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futenma|1=Futenma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11514557}}, Ginowan, Okinawa Prefecture |latitude = 26.292750 |longitude = 127.776806 |map_type = Japan Okinawa main island#Japan Okinawa Prefecture |mountain = Futenmazan |denomination = {{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon-shū|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10343918}} |temple_Rank = {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Futenma Gongen|ja|普天満権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futenma Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 普天満宮|1=Futemma-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11514556}} |principal_image = Shōkanzeon Bosatsu (Avalokiteśvara) |official_name = 暜天滿山神宮寺
Futenmazan Jingū-ji }} '''Jingū-ji''' (神宮寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|ja|東寺真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji Shingon-shū|1=Tōji Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10343918}} sect of Buddhism located in {{ill|Futenma|ja|普天間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futenma|1=Futenma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11514557}}, Ginowan, Okinawa Prefecture.Formerly {{ill|Futenma Village|ja|普天間村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futenma Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nakagami District, Okinawa|en|Nakagami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1205862|lt=Nakagami District|1=Nakagami District, Okinawa}}. The temple's mountain name (sangō) is '''Futenmazan''' (普天満山), and the principal object of worship is {{ill|Shōkanzeon Bosatsu|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkanzeon Bosatsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}} (Avalokiteśvara). Prior to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (Separation of Shinto and Buddhism), it was the {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (affiliated temple) of the adjacent {{ill|Futemma-gū|lt=Futemma-gū|lt=Futenma Shrine|ja|普天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futenma Shrine|qq=}}. == History == Established in the mid-15th century by royal order of King {{ill|Shō Taikyū|en|Shō Taikyū|qid=Q1139919|lt=Shō Taikyū|1=Shō Taikyū}} as a prayer site for local residents. It is said that a resting place at Futenma during inspection tours from {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|qid=Q608415|lt=Shuri|1=Shuri, Okinawa}} to the central region gradually developed into the temple. According to another oral tradition, when {{ill|Gosamaru|en|Gosamaru|qid=Q3809442|lt=Gosamaru|1=Gosamaru}}, the lord of {{ill|Nakagusuku Castle|en|Nakagusuku Castle|qid=Q1013209|lt=Nakagusuku Castle|1=Nakagusuku Castle}}, was attacked and defeated by {{ill|Amawari|en|Amawari|qid=Q4740706|lt=Amawari|1=Amawari}}, lord of {{ill|Katsuren Castle|en|Katsuren Castle|qid=Q2471589|lt=Katsuren Castle|1=Katsuren Castle}}, the construction materials were brought here, and the temple was built to honor Gosamaru's memory. == Transportation == Accessible by {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|qid=Q1373492|lt=route bus|1=Transit bus}} (disembark at Futenma Bus Stop) or {{ill|Taxi|en|Taxi|qid=Q82650|lt=taxi|1=Taxi}}. {{Main|Bus routes on Okinawa Main Island}} * 21 (Shintoshin-Gushikawa Line) - {{ill|Ryukyu Bus Kotsu|en|Ryukyu Bus Kotsu|qid=Q11573640|lt=Ryukyu Bus Kotsu|1=Ryukyu Bus Kotsu}} * 23 (Gushikawa Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu * 25 (Futenma Airport Line) - {{ill|Naha Bus|ja|那覇バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naha Bus|1=Naha Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642811}} * 27 (Yakena Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu / {{ill|Okinawa Bus|ja|沖縄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawa Bus|1=Okinawa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11552619}} * 31 (Awase West Line) - {{ill|Toyo Bus|en|Toyo Bus|qid=Q11528673|lt=Toyo Bus|1=Toyo Bus}} * 52 (Yokatsu Line) - Okinawa Bus * 58 (Baten-Ryudai-Awase Line) - Toyo Bus * 61 (Maehara Line) - Okinawa Bus * 77 (Nago East Line) - Okinawa Bus * 80 (Yonashiro Line) - Okinawa Bus * 88 (Ginowan Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu * 90 (Chibana Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu * 110 (Nagata-Gushikawa Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu * 223 (Gushikawa-Omoromachi Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu * 227 (Yakena-Omoromachi Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu / Okinawa Bus * 290 (Chibana-Omoromachi Line) - Ryukyu Bus Kotsu == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://futenma-jinguji.com/ Official website of Futenmazan Jingū-ji] * [http://www.kagemarukun.fromc.jp/page125b.html Jingū-ji information page] {{pref-stub-Okinawa Prefecture}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Okinawan Shrines}}]] {{translated page|ja|神宮寺 (宜野湾市)|version=94239283|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神宮寺 (宜野湾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11589476}} [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:chokugan-ji]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ginowan]] [[Category:Tōji Shingon temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
